company_core.name: Distyl AI
company_core.description: Distyl AI provides a platform that automates the creation and deployment of AI-driven workflows, utilizing generative AI models in conjunction with existing organizational data. The technology emphasizes transparency and auditability, enabling the customization of workflows to align with specific business needs.

founders.name: Arjun Prakash
founders.background:
Arjun Prakash has extensive experience in the tech industry, having served as Head of Solutions at Snorkel AI, where he led new product development and business strategy. Prior to that, he was an early executive at Palantir Technologies, contributing to the scaling of the commercial business over eight years. He also worked as an Associate at Blackrock Healthcare Group, developing quantitative models, and conducted research at Siemens, focusing on pattern recognition for medical imaging. Arjun holds a BS in Electrical and Computer Engineering from Cornell University and has furthered his education at Cornell Tech.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/arjunsprakash, twitter.com/arjunsprakash

founders.name: Derek Ho
founders.background:
Derek Ho has a strong background in business development and finance, having worked at Palantir Technologies from 2017 to 2022, where he managed C-suite relationships and drove digital transformation initiatives. Prior to that, he was a Fixed Income Trader at Citadel (2013-2016) and Credit Suisse (2010-2013), where he developed quantitative models to enhance profitability. He holds a BSc in Finance, Accounting, and Statistics from The Wharton School.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/derek-ho-distyl

agent_output.markdown:
# Distyl AI | Meeting Brief
*2026-03-03*

## TL;DR
Distyl AI is a B2B enterprise AI workflow automation platform that just closed a massive $175M Series B in September 2025, bringing total funding to $202M. [[1]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)[[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692) The company is backed by a strong investor syndicate including Lightspeed, Coatue, and Dell Technologies, and is showing healthy growth signals across headcount and traffic. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692) The founding team carries deep Palantir pedigree, which is a meaningful signal for enterprise go-to-market credibility.

---

## Company Overview
Distyl AI automates the creation and deployment of AI-driven workflows using generative AI models integrated with existing organizational data. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692) The platform emphasizes transparency and auditability — a differentiator relevant to regulated industries. The company operates in healthcare/life sciences, analytics, and developer tooling verticals. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)

- **Founded:** 2022 [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)
- **Stage:** Series B [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)
- **Total Raised:** $202M [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)
- **Headcount:** 124 (+12.7% over 90 days) [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)
- **Web Traffic:** 14,870 monthly visitors (+4.8% over 30 days) [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)

---

## Key Points
• **Massive Series B signals strong conviction:** The $175M round closed September 23, 2025 — an unusually large Series B that suggests investors see significant enterprise traction or a winner-take-most dynamic in AI workflow automation. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)

• **Blue-chip investor syndicate:** Backed by Lightspeed Venture Partners, Coatue Management, Dell Technologies, Millennium Technology Value Partners, and AI Grant. Dell's participation is notable and may indicate a strategic distribution or infrastructure partnership angle. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)

• **Palantir-heavy founding team is a genuine asset:** CEO Arjun Prakash spent eight years as an early executive at Palantir scaling the commercial business, and COO Derek Ho also came from Palantir (2017–2022) managing C-suite relationships and digital transformation. [[3]](https://swarm.com) This is a team that knows how to sell complex data products to large enterprises.

• **Transparency and auditability as core differentiators:** The platform is explicitly built around auditable, customizable workflows — a critical requirement in healthcare and regulated industries where the company appears to be targeting. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)

• **Headcount growth is accelerating:** 12.7% headcount growth in 90 days on a base of 124 employees suggests active hiring across functions post-fundraise. [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)

---

## Recent Developments
• Closed $175M Series B on September 23, 2025, the largest round in the company's history. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)
• Headcount has grown 12.7% in the past 90 days, indicating rapid post-funding team expansion. [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)
• Web traffic up 4.8% in the past 30 days, suggesting growing market awareness or inbound demand. [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)

---

## Signals & Trends
• **Hiring surge:** 12.7% headcount growth in 90 days is well above typical growth rates and points to aggressive scaling — worth understanding which functions are being built out (sales, engineering, GTM). [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)
• **Traffic growth is modest but positive:** 14,870 monthly visitors with 4.8% 30-day growth is relatively low for a $202M-funded company — this may reflect a direct enterprise sales motion rather than inbound-led growth, or could indicate limited brand awareness. [[4]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)
• **Healthcare/life sciences focus:** The company's vertical tags suggest a deliberate focus on regulated industries where auditability requirements create a natural moat. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)

---

## For This Meeting
**Key questions to ask:**
• What is the current ARR and revenue growth rate — does it justify the $175M Series B valuation?
• Who are the anchor enterprise customers, and what does the typical contract size and sales cycle look like?
• How is the Palantir playbook being applied here — is this a services-heavy or product-led motion?
• What specific roles are being hired for with the new capital, and what does the 18-month roadmap look like?
• How does Dell Technologies' involvement translate into distribution or go-to-market leverage?

**Risks to probe:**
• Low web traffic relative to funding raised could indicate heavy reliance on a small number of large customers — concentration risk.
• The AI workflow automation space is crowded; press for specific differentiation beyond "transparency and auditability."
• Palantir alumni are excellent at enterprise sales but can build operationally complex, services-heavy businesses — clarify the product vs. services revenue mix.

**Areas needing clarification:**
• No third co-founder or CTO identified in available data — worth understanding the technical leadership structure. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)[[3]](https://swarm.com)
• No specific customer names, case studies, or revenue metrics available in provided data. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)
• The role of AI Grant in the cap table is unusual alongside institutional investors — worth understanding the context. [[2]](https://console.harmonic.ai/companies/11533692)
